Status Snapshot

Overview Status Snapshot Source Matrix Eval And Audit Status Demo Queries Limitations And Public-Alpha Boundaries Live-Alpha Preparation Roadmap Local Quickstart
This is a generated static snapshot from the local repo. It does not make live status calls.

Runtime

Python remains the active reference/oracle lane. The Rust parity lane now has isolated source-registry and query-planner candidates, but Rust is not the active runtime.

Public Alpha

Public Alpha Safe Mode exists and blocks caller-provided local path controls in constrained demo routes. Public Alpha Rehearsal Evidence v0 records local static-site, smoke, route, eval, and baseline-status evidence. LIVE_ALPHA_01 adds a public-alpha wrapper with localhost defaults, closed live-probe gates, and config checks. Public alpha is not production and is not open live hosting approval.

Archive Evals

The Archive Resolution Eval Runner currently reports 6 hard tasks internally satisfied against the fixture-backed local corpus. Internal satisfaction is regression evidence, not a production relevance benchmark.

Search Usefulness

The current local audit reports 64 queries: 5 covered, 22 partial, 26 source gaps, 9 capability gaps, and 2 unknown. Source and capability gaps remain expected.

External Baselines

Google Web Search, Internet Archive metadata search, and Internet Archive full-text/OCR search remain external baselines pending/manual. Batch 0 has 39 pending slots and 0 observed records.

Static Public Data

Generated Public Data Summaries v0 publishes deterministic JSON summaries under data/. Lite/Text/Files Seed Surfaces v0 consumes those summaries for no-JS/no-download compatibility surfaces, Static Resolver Demo Snapshots v0 adds fixture-backed static demo examples under demo/, Custom Domain / Alternate Host Readiness v0 adds static-host portability contracts without configuring DNS, CNAME, or an alternate host, Live Backend Handoff Contract v0 reserves /api/v1 as future contract-only handoff routes, Live Probe Gateway Contract v0 defines disabled-by-default source probe policy without implementing probes, Rust Query Planner Parity Candidate v0 adds an isolated Rust planner candidate without runtime wiring, and Compatibility Surface Strategy v0 records the cross-surface plan for web, lite, text, files, data, API, snapshots, relay, CLI, and future native clients without adding runtime behavior. Signed Snapshot Format v0 now defines a repo-local seed snapshot format and checksum/signature-placeholder policy without adding production signatures, keys, downloads, relay behavior, native clients, or a public /snapshots/ route. Relay Surface Design v0 records future local/LAN bridge policy, security/privacy defaults, and an unsigned operator checklist without adding a relay runtime, protocol server, network listener, private data exposure, live-probe passthrough, or write/admin path.

  • Machine-readable site summary
  • Machine-readable page registry
  • Lite HTML surface
  • Text surface
  • Files surface
  • Static resolver demos

Deferred Surfaces

The public /snapshots/ route, production signed snapshots, relay runtime, native clients, the rich /app surface, and live /api/v1 remain future or contract-only; the repo-local snapshot seed example is not a public download surface. Backend deployment infrastructure is absent; /api/v1 is not live, no custom domain is configured, Google remains manual-baseline only, and no live source probes, Internet Archive live calls, source crawling, auth, accounts, rate limiting, TLS ownership, or production process supervision are implemented here.

Eureka is a Python reference backend prototype, not production. This site uses no scraping, performs no live source probes, and makes no external search calls. external baselines pending/manual; source placeholders remain placeholders.
